Document:
# Thaven
[color=#808080]Thaven are [color=#b3b3b3]young adults[/color] until age 50, [color=#b3b3b3]adults[/color] until age 100, [color=#b3b3b3]middle-aged[/color] until age 150, [color=#b3b3b3]older[/color] until age 200, and [color=#b3b3b3]elderly[/color] beyond that point.[/color]
## Moods Thaven have an unique brain structure that's susceptible to ionizing radiation, and thus have "moods" which incite them to change the way they behave.
All thaven naturally have and are affected by moods. Through RP and sufficiently reasonable character building, the severity of moods may differ from character to character, such as an ion-shielded skull dampening the effect of storms on the character's brain, a harsh regimen of mood stabilizers giving them a more even-keeled demeanor, or direct brain implants regulating the user's emotional and mental state. Remember, the reasons for obtaining and having implants like these are expected to be realistic and grounded for the character and should not just be excuses to avoid playing out moods.
[color=red]Note that a thaven's moods as written are non-diegetic gameplay features.[/color] They should be treated as things that have been integrated into your character's personality, and should not be referenced by their specific names or descriptions. You may choose for your character to be aware that their behavior has changed since their last shift, but they should not be able to recite exactly what is presented to you, the player. [color=#1e90ff]Thaven are, however, fully aware of this phenomenon, and may appropriately refer to them as "moods".[/color]
[color=#ffa500]These moods should be followed by your character, however basic morals override said moods![/color] A mood being violated by yourself or others can range from a small amount of discomfort all the way to a cascading mental breakdown, with proper roleplay. The specifics of that are up to you, but if a mood is violated, you should seek to correct the mistake as to relieve your character's mental state.
[color=red]Following your moods does NOT afford you a license to do things which would otherwise be against the rules. Always refer to escalation rules, and AHelp (F1) if you have any questions.[/color]
## Mood Swings Thaven brains are also susceptible to fluctuations in electromagnetic fields. As a result, their ability to synchronize and regulate their moods tends to degrade with extended exposure to the cosmic radiation and ion storms that permeate space stations.
While thaven naturally have moods, they also evolved to live in the protective magnetosphere of a planet. Life in space presents this unique challenge for them. How your character chooses to respond to this fact is up to you.
## Physiology The highly specialized endocrine system of the Thaven is capable of emitting a small amount of toxin from their skin, coupled with standardized defense courses in all Thaven hatching academies allows [color=#ffa500]their unarmed attacks to slowly build stun in their opponents by a mix of nerve pinching and toxin buildup.[/color]
Due to sharing a common ancestor with space carp, thaven lungs are strange, resilient organs needed for blood circulation rather than gas exchange, so [color=#ffa500]inhaled gases have no effect on them.[/color] As such, [color=#1e90ff]thaven can safely breathe any kind of atmosphere,[/color] though they [color=#ffa500]remain vulnerable to pressure extremes and barotrauma.[/color]
Note that this also affects thaven undergoing surgery: [color=red]nitrous oxide won't work on them, and surgeons may have to resort to chloral hydrate.[/color]
All thaven organs contain traces of neurons, and because of this, they take [color=#ffa500]10% more brute and cellular damage, and 20% more radiation damage.[/color] However, their unique ancestry allows them to take [color=#1e90ff]10% less heat, cold, shock, and poison damage.[/color]
Like space carp, thaven flesh and organs contain carpotoxin. Their blood, while nontoxic, is [color=#ffa500]powerfully hallucinogenic.[/color]
Thaven mature at the same age as humans, but they live roughly twice as long. Culturally, their lives are generally measured in fifths, with each being about fifty standard years long.
## Language Thaven speak Eckeckyik, a sing-song language quite similar to the calls and chirps of Earth's (now mostly extinct) dolphins and whales.
The language consists of long vocalizations, clicks, chirps, and subtle manipulation of acoustic resonance to allow for clear communication while underwater. A large emphasis is placed on subsonic frequency and harmonics, making the language nearly impossible for other species to pick up.
Outsiders often describe the language as a beautifully strange symphony of sound, though some label it as a blaring cacophony of annoying squeaking and squealing.
Multiple dialects of Eckeckyik have been recognized across Thaven culture, while most still make use of their unique ability to manipulate subsonics- some dialects, like Bieselized Eckeckyik, have been "humanized" by close proximity to other cultures influence, creating a more speakable tongue for species without thaven vocal cords.
And in every relevant language, the plural of thaven is thaven.
## Culture
Due to their unique psychology Thaven culture differs wildly from group to group. While the vast majority of the Thaven population falls under Tau Ceti space, and thereby Biesel Government, a small few sects have held out across the further reaches of the territory. The remaining "Free Thaven" governments and civilizations are generally quite small, with some being dwindling technological giants to others remaining simple frontier kelp aquafarming villages. Within the last century and a half, the remaining "Free Thaven" governments have started to observe a sharp drop both economic and population growth, having been attributed to the growing scarcity of phoron, and a strange increase in the local sectors cosmic radiation levels causing increased instability among thaven youth.
Shared across all Thaven population, isolationist frontier planets or Biesel's own Little Eckeyik district, is a great love for Kelp Pods! While the origin of the plant and how it has spread so far through Thaven culture is unknown, it remains a staple and beloved part of all Thaven households! Traditionally, the kelp pod is grown year-round for its medicinal properties, with small crops being used for fermentation and culinary purposes. Some deep sea villages have even been reported to selectively breed bioluminescent pods to be used as light sources!
## Thaven Naming Conventions
Thaven typically name themselves after a single virtue they hold dear. Sometimes these names can be long and unwieldy. Certain cultures have differing conventions in naming due to differences in what concepts or personal attributes are considered important or admirable. Thaven raised among their own may have names given by clutchsitters or other senior family members, while more diasporic Thaven may entirely forego the traditional naming process in favor of something more "human".
Below is a list of some common names used in the broader collective of Thaven culture, alongside some that might be considered inappropriate to use in the setting.
- [color=#449944]Appropriate:[/color] Honesty - [color=#449944]Appropriate:[/color] Have Mercy - [color=#449944]Appropriate:[/color] Give Thanks To Thy Ancestors - [color=#449944]Appropriate:[/color] Obedience - [color=#449944]Appropriate:[/color] Search The Scriptures - [color=#449944]Appropriate:[/color] Always Hold Love In Thy Heart - [color=#449944]Appropriate:[/color] Journey Before Destination - [color=#994444]Inappropriate:[/color] John Smith - [color=#994444]Inappropriate:[/color] Fuck The Police - [color=#994444]Inappropriate:[/color] Live Long And Prosper - [color=#994444]Inappropriate:[/color] Always Be Diggin In Ya Butt - [color=#994444]Inappropriate:[/color] Fuck Bitches Get Money - [color=#994444]Inappropriate:[/color] Confirm Heterosexuality By Doing Your Mother - [color=#994444]Inappropriate:[/color] Shitting In Subways
Below are some examples of more background specific names to give better insight into how fringe Thaven cultures might differ in convention.
Due to their uniquely character specific nature, please do not try to use any of these examples in your own character creation process.
- [color=Yellow]Background Specific:[/color] Remain Incompetent Forever - [color=Yellow]Background Specific:[/color] The Ever Churning Tide - [color=Yellow]Background Specific:[/color] Always Make Good Decisions
When making a culturally isolated Thaven, be that separated from other Thavens or isolated from the greater influence of the spur, remember to put extra thought into the backstory of your character and why they might be different from others of their kind. Remember, Thavens are held to stricter RP standard than other species available to play. Make sure you know your character before putting yourself in too deep of a role.
These 3 examples show options for: A Thaven raised by humans on Biesel, A Thaven committed to the Hadiist ideals of the People's Republic, and a Thaven that has thrown out their birthname and culture to make their own path in life.
- [color=Red]VERY Background Specific:[/color] Ake "Fishsticks" Ewo - [color=Red]VERY Background Specific:[/color] Cultivate Thy Inner Plasteel, The Dogs Of War Grow Hungry - [color=Red]VERY Background Specific:[/color] Forsake The Names Of Thy Fathers, Burn Thy Own Path In Life
